name: claude-hooks-task-state-machine
tags: task status, state machine, transitions, valid statuses, is_valid_transition, open_tasks
body:
Task status state machine, verified against src/tools/tasks.py (_VALID_STATUSES, _TRANSITIONS, is_valid_transition):

_VALID_STATUSES = {open, done, abandoned, blocked} — 'active' is NOT a DB status, see [[tasks-active-status-checkpoint-only]].

_TRANSITIONS:
  open    → {done, blocked}
  done    → {} (terminal)
  abandoned → {} (terminal)
  blocked → {open}

Any status → abandoned is always allowed (special-cased in is_valid_transition, not in the _TRANSITIONS dict). Same-status is always a no-op allow.

handle_update() in src/tools/tasks.py is the ONLY path for status transitions — it calls is_valid_transition() to enforce the machine. Nodes must NEVER run `UPDATE open_tasks SET status=...` directly — bypasses the guard and breaks gate enforcement.

**Correction (2026-07-05):** previous version of this memory claimed "open → active → done" and "active → open (requeue)" / "blocked → active (unblock)" — 'active' has never been a DB status; that was drift from confusing the DB status machine with the LangGraph checkpoint's active_task_id tracking. Verified directly against the _TRANSITIONS dict in code, not against another memory.
